title: Childhood Vaccination Coverage Equity Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring sub-national vaccination coverage equity gaps, linking DHS and MICS immunization data, wealth-quintile disaggregation, and health-facility data to identify zero-dose children.
why_it_matters: Immunization prevents 4 million deaths annually. Yet 20 million children remain unvaccinated or under-vaccinated, concentrated in the poorest wealth quintiles and remote districts. The Immunization Agenda 2030 targets zero-dose reduction, but measuring where zero-dose children live and why they are missed requires coverage-equity analysis that is underdeveloped at sub-national scale.

success_metrics.target: Every coverage-equity estimate links survey data, wealth disaggregation, and facility data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
